2604.21766.title: AUDITA: A New Dataset to Audit Humans vs. AI Skill at Audio QA
2604.21766.paper_summary: Existing audio question answering benchmarks largely emphasize sound event classification or caption-grounded queries, often enabling models to succeed through shortcut strategies, short-duration cues, lexical priors, dataset-specific biases, or even bypassing audio via metadata and captions rather than genuine reasoning Thus, we present AUDITA (Audio Understanding from Diverse Internet Trivia Authors), a large-scale, real-world benchmark to rigorously evaluate audio reasoning beyond surface-level acoustic recognition. AUDITA comprises carefully curated, human-authored trivia questions grounded in real-world audio, designed to stress robust auditory reasoning through challenging distractors and long-range temporal dependencies, using probing queries that cannot be answered from isolated text or sound cues alone. Human average accuracy of 32.13% shows both the challenge of the task while demonstrating meaningful comprehension of the audio. In stark contrast, state of-the-art audio question answering models perform poorly, with average accuracy below 8.86%. Beyond raw accuracy, we apply Item Response Theory (IRT) to estimate latent proficiency, question difficulty, and expose systematic deficiencies of the models and data.
2604.21766.paper_summary_zh: 現有的音頻問答基準主要強調聲音事件分類或基於標題的查詢，這往往使模型能夠通過捷徑策略、短時提示、詞彙先驗、數據集特定偏見，甚至通過元數據和標題而非真正的推理來成功。因此，我們提出了AUDITA（來自多元互聯網趣聞作者的音頻理解），這是一個大規模的現實世界基準，旨在嚴格評估超越表面聲學識別的音頻推理。AUDITA包含精心策劃的人類撰寫的趣味問題，這些問題基於現實世界的音頻，旨在通過具有挑戰性的干擾項和長期時間依賴性來強調穩健的聽覺推理，使用無法僅從孤立文本或聲音提示中回答的探測性查詢。人類的平均準確率為32.13%，顯示了這項任務的挑戰，同時展示了對音頻的有意義理解。相比之下，最先進的音頻問答模型表現不佳，平均準確率低於8.86%。除了原始準確率外，我們應用項目反應理論（IRT）來估計潛在能力、問題難度，並揭示模型和數據的系統性缺陷。
2604.21766.author: Tasnim Kabir et.al.
2604.21766.authors: Tasnim Kabir, Dmytro Kurdydyk, Aadi Palnitkar, Liam Dorn, Ahmed Haj Ahmed, Jordan Lee Boyd-Graber
